message:
fuzz: add webhook_signature + bootstrap_recovery targets with seed corpora

Lands the two new libfuzzer harnesses whose Cargo.toml entries were
already declared in prior commits but whose target files + seed
corpora were never actually checked in. Without these files the
cargo-fuzz build fails on a fresh clone, so this commit restores
the declared-vs-actual invariant of fuzz/Cargo.toml.

fuzz_targets/webhook_signature.rs — covers three orthogonal attack
surfaces every real-world webhook receiver must defend:
1. Stripe Stripe-Signature header parser (comma-separated k/v grammar
   that funnels into an HMAC verifier - a crash or infinite loop here
   is a pre-auth DoS primitive).
2. Stripe end-to-end verify_and_parse with arbitrary headers and body,
   exercising header parsing, timestamp handling, HMAC verification,
   JSON parsing, and event construction.
3. GitHub verify_and_parse end-to-end with the sha256= prefix parsing,
   case-insensitive header lookup, and hex-decoded HMAC-SHA256 verify.
Also asserts a positive invariant: a freshly minted HmacSha256Verifier
signature must verify against the same key/body, and must fail to
verify if a single byte of either key or body is mutated - giving the
fuzzer coverage of the success path, not just the rejection path.
MAX_INPUT_BYTES = 8 KiB to keep per-input work bounded.

fuzz_targets/bootstrap_recovery.rs — exercises the PIN/mnemonic
bootstrap recovery path: empty, equal, differing, long, non-UTF-8,
and null-byte-containing PINs; mnemonics with bogus words, wrong
counts, extra whitespace; and structurally malformed inputs with no
separators or null bytes.

Seed corpora are small hand-crafted inputs covering the interesting
grammar edges (valid shapes, invalid hex, missing fields, oversized
sigs, multi-v1 Stripe entries) so libfuzzer starts from meaningful
coverage rather than from /dev/null.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
